claim: Certifies the exact oriented-block factorization of the ordered-branch residual of Q_w for w=12,16.
claim_boundary: This is a normal-form/factorization certificate, not the final proof of residual nonnegativity under the barrier.

theorem.compact_formula: Equivalently Q_res,w = sum_{T in O_w} x_T*x0*((w-1)*x0^2-(w+1)*x1^2-R).
theorem.formula_ordered_branch: With x0=x1+c, b=x1, and R=sum_{i=2}^{23}x_i^2, Q_res,w = sum_{T in O_w} x_T[-2b^3+(2w-4)cb^2+(3w-3)c^2b+(w-1)c^3-(b+c)R].
theorem.interpretation: The residual is not arbitrary. It is supported exactly on oriented pair-asymmetric blocks and has one universal cubic template per block.
theorem.name: Q-residual oriented block factorization
theorem.remaining_target: Use the barrier condition to prove this oriented-block weighted sum is nonnegative, or absorb the compact residual into a barrier multiplier plus ordered-cone-positive terms.
theorem.statement: The ordered-branch residual of Q_w factors over the oriented block-difference family O_w={T: {0} union T is a shell block and 1 notin block}.
